_comment_top: Codex hooks — rendered from outcome/cli-configs/codex/hooks.json at /init. Mirror of the format-edit hook in claude/settings.json so both CLIs apply the formatter consistently.

UserPromptSubmit._comment: Mirror of claude/settings.json bootstrap gate. Note: Codex CLI's UserPromptSubmit hook support may vary by version — if your Codex version doesn't recognize this hook event, it will silently ignore it (graceful degradation). The standalone-bootstrap.md flow + the Bootstrap Status Check block in CLAUDE.md still cover this scenario via Claude Code; Codex-only sessions on an unconfigured project may need the user to manually invoke the bootstrap.

Mirror of claude/settings.json budget pressure monitor (P1.6). Reads transcript size, emits advisory additionalContext at 50%/75%/90% thresholds. Codex may not implement transcript_path semantics identically; if the hook can't parse stdin, it no-ops silently (harmless). Configurable via CCC_CONTEXT_BUDGET env var (default 200000).

PostToolUse._comment: Run formatter on every Edit/Write. The format-edit.sh script picks the right formatter based on file extension.

SessionStart._comment: Mirror of claude/settings.json memory-recall hook. NOTE: Codex CLI's SessionStart hook support may vary by version — if your Codex version doesn't recognize this event, it will silently ignore the entry (graceful degradation). Memory recall works fully under Claude Code; Codex-only sessions on the same project still benefit because the manual /remember skill still writes to the same observations.jsonl.

PreCompaction._comment: Mirror of claude/settings.json memory-snapshot hook. NOTE: same Codex-version caveat as SessionStart above — if PreCompaction is not implemented by your Codex version, this entry silently no-ops.
